name: @atproto/api
what_it_does: The official AT Protocol client SDK: Agent, RichText, and the Moderation API, with typed access to the com.atproto and app.bsky lexicons. The default starting point for building AT Protocol apps.

name: @atproto/oauth-client-browser
what_it_does: Official browser OAuth client (DPoP proof-of-possession, Client-ID Metadata Document). App Passwords are being phased out in favour of OAuth.

name: @atproto/oauth-client-node
what_it_does: Official Node OAuth client for AT Protocol with DPoP and dynamic client registration.

name: @atproto/identity
what_it_does: Handle and DID resolution for AT Protocol (HandleResolver, DidResolver) with caching.

name: @atproto/repo
what_it_does: AT Protocol repositories: the Merkle Search Tree (MST) and signed CBOR records.

name: @atproto/lexicon
what_it_does: Lexicon schema validation (assertValidRecord / assertValidXrpcParams / assertValidXrpcOutput).

name: @atproto/crypto
what_it_does: P-256 (secp256r1) and K-256 (secp256k1) signing, SHA-256, and did:key — the cryptography under AT Protocol identity.

name: @atcute/client
what_it_does: atcute: a modular, edge-focused, lightweight AT Protocol client (community, by mary-ext) — a leaner alternative to @atproto/api.

name: @atcute/oauth-browser-client
what_it_does: Community browser OAuth client from the atcute project.

name: indigo
what_it_does: The Go monorepo: Relay (cmd/relay — the firehose, formerly 'BGS', informally targeting ~100M accounts and tens of thousands of repo events/sec at peak), rainbow (firehose fan-out), tap (sync/backfill), hepa (auto-moderation for Ozone), and goat (CLI).

name: jetstream
what_it_does: Converts the AT Protocol firehose (CBOR/CAR) into lightweight JSON over WebSocket — zstd dictionary compression, ≤100 collections / ≤10k DIDs. The easy ingestion path for feeds, bots, labelers, and prototyping. Public instances on bsky.network.

name: did-method-plc
what_it_does: The did:plc identity method: SHA-256 of the genesis operation, base32, 24 chars; an append-only, auditable transparent log supporting key rotation and account recovery.

name: social-app
what_it_does: Bluesky's official Web/iOS/Android client (React Native + Expo), built on @atproto/api and the app.bsky lexicons — a reference for building an AT Protocol client.

name: ozone
what_it_does: AT Protocol moderation tooling: a Next.js UI + service + PostgreSQL via Kysely (not Prisma); stackable, opt-in labelers (tools.ozone.*). Security: pin React ≥19.1.2 (CVE-2025-55182) and keep your Docker update tag in sync.

name: feed-generator
what_it_does: The official custom-feed starter kit: Express + SQLite + Kysely implementing getFeedSkeleton with a skeleton/hydration architecture.

name: pds
what_it_does: Self-host a Personal Data Server: container + compose + installer (~1 GB RAM, ~20 GB SSD); Caddy TLS + Watchtower; SQLite-per-repo with Litestream backups. Needs wildcard DNS and the subscribeRepos WebSocket reachable.

name: atrium-api
what_it_does: atrium: the Rust AT Protocol SDK (atrium-api / -xrpc / -oauth / -cli), with lexicon codegen via lexgen. Actively published on crates.io.

name: atproto
what_it_does: atproto.dart: the core Dart/Flutter AT Protocol SDK (by Shinya Kato); used by SkyFeed and deck.blue. Pairs with the high-level 'bluesky' package.

name: atproto
what_it_does: MarshalX/atproto: the complete, typed Python AT Protocol SDK — firehose consumption, Pydantic v2 models, and Rust-backed DAG-CBOR/CAR. Python 3.9–3.14.

name: minisky
what_it_does: minisky: a minimal Ruby AT Protocol / Bluesky client (by mackuba).

name: granary
what_it_does: granary: a data-format translator between HTML/mf2/RSS/Atom/AS1/AS2 and AT Protocol lexicons (by snarfed). The engine under Bridgy Fed.

name: ATProtoKit
what_it_does: ATProtoKit: the Swift AT Protocol SDK (by Christopher Jr Riley) — very active.

name: bskyr
what_it_does: bskyr: access AT Protocol / Bluesky from R, with tidy tibble outputs.

name: idunno.Bluesky
what_it_does: idunno.Bluesky: a .NET / C# AT Protocol client (by Barry Dorrans).

name: bridgy-fed
what_it_does: Bridgy Fed: bridges ActivityPub ↔ AT Protocol ↔ IndieWeb (by snarfed), built on granary and arroba (an independent PDS implementation).

name: WhiteWind
what_it_does: WhiteWind: long-form Markdown blogging on AT Protocol (com.whtwnd.*) — a Go XRPC backend + Next.js frontend.

name: Tangled
what_it_does: Tangled: git collaboration on AT Protocol ('knots' + an AppView; sh.tangled.*).

name: Leaflet
what_it_does: Leaflet: collaborative documents and social publishing on AT Protocol (alpha).

name: Grain
what_it_does: Grain: photo sharing on AT Protocol (Deno/TypeScript AppView + Rust microservices; alpha).

name: Frontpage
what_it_does: Frontpage: a Hacker News-style link-aggregator AppView on AT Protocol.

name: Smoke Signal
what_it_does: Smoke Signal: events and RSVP management on AT Protocol.

name: Streamplace
what_it_does: Streamplace: live video on AT Protocol.

name: PinkSea
what_it_does: PinkSea: an oekaki (collaborative drawing) BBS on AT Protocol.

name: ClearSky
what_it_does: ClearSky: block and blocked-by lists for AT Protocol (third-party; verify at use).

name: rsky
what_it_does: rsky: BlackSky's Rust implementation of AT Protocol services (PDS, feed generators, and more) — open infrastructure built by and for the Black community on Bluesky.

name: BlackSky Algorithms
what_it_does: BlackSky Algorithms: the open-source GitHub org for BlackSky's AT Protocol feeds, relays, and tooling — community infrastructure for AT Protocol builders.

name: BlackSky Services Directory
what_it_does: BlackSky community services directory: the full list of relays, feeds, and tools the BlackSky community runs for AT Protocol builders.

name: BlackSky Forge
what_it_does: BlackSky Forge: the community's self-hosted git forge (blackskyweb.xyz) where its AT Protocol services are developed.

name: BlackSky Move
what_it_does: BlackSky Move: an account-migration / onboarding service in the BlackSky AT Protocol stack.

name: BlackSky Acorn
what_it_does: BlackSky Acorn: a community service in the BlackSky AT Protocol stack for builders.
